English Sentence: I wake up at seven o'clock every morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每天早上七點起床。

English Sentence: She brushes her teeth before going to bed.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她睡覺前會刷牙。

English Sentence: We have breakfast together as a family every weekend.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們每個週末都會全家一起吃早餐。

English Sentence: He takes the bus to work because he doesn't have a car.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他搭公車上班，因為他沒有車。

English Sentence: I usually cook dinner at home, but sometimes I order takeaway.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我通常在家做晚飯，但有時候會點外賣。
Grammar / Usage Note: contrast using but with frequency adverbs

English Sentence: She goes for a walk in the park every evening after dinner.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每天晚飯後都會去公園散步。

English Sentence: He spends about an hour reading before he falls asleep.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他睡著前大約會花一個小時閱讀。

English Sentence: I do the laundry on Sundays and clean the bathroom on Saturdays.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我週日洗衣服，週六打掃浴室。

English Sentence: They always have a family dinner on Friday nights.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他們每週五晚上都會一起吃家庭晚餐。
Grammar / Usage Note: frequency adverb always with present simple

English Sentence: She checks her emails first thing every morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每天早上第一件事就是查電子郵件。

English Sentence: I pack my lunch the night before to save time in the morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我前一天晚上就把午餐準備好，以節省早上的時間。

English Sentence: He walks the dog twice a day, once in the morning and once in the evening.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他每天遛狗兩次，早上一次，晚上一次。

English Sentence: We try to eat dinner together at the table rather than in front of the TV.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們盡量在餐桌上一起吃晚飯，而不是在電視前面。

English Sentence: She listens to podcasts while she commutes to work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她通勤上班時會聽播客。
Grammar / Usage Note: while + present simple for simultaneous actions

English Sentence: I set three alarms because I'm worried about oversleeping.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我設了三個鬧鐘，因為我擔心睡過頭。

English Sentence: He always feels better after a hot shower in the morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他早上洗完熱水澡後總是感覺好多了。

English Sentence: She keeps a journal and writes in it every night before bed.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她有寫日記的習慣，每天晚上睡前都會寫。

English Sentence: I spend my lunch break going for a short walk to clear my head.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我利用午休時間去短暫散步，讓頭腦清醒。
Grammar / Usage Note: gerund phrase to clear my head

English Sentence: We take turns cooking dinner so that neither of us gets too tired.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們輪流做晚飯，這樣就不會有人太累了。
Grammar / Usage Note: take turns + gerund, so that clause

English Sentence: He rarely eats fast food because he prefers home-cooked meals.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他很少吃速食，因為他更喜歡自己做的飯。

English Sentence: I try to drink at least eight glasses of water a day.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每天嘗試至少喝八杯水。
Grammar / Usage Note: try to + infinitive with at least

English Sentence: She volunteers at the local food bank every other Saturday.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每隔一個週六都會在當地的食物銀行做志工。

English Sentence: We go to the farmers market on weekend mornings to buy fresh produce.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們週末早上去農夫市集買新鮮農產品。

English Sentence: He keeps his workspace tidy so that he can focus better.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他保持工作區整潔，這樣可以更好地專注。

English Sentence: I try to limit my screen time to two hours a day outside of work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我盡量將工作以外的螢幕時間限制在每天兩小時。

English Sentence: She has long dark hair and bright green eyes.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她有一頭深色長髮和明亮的綠眼睛。

English Sentence: He is quite tall, probably around 185 centimetres.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他相當高，大概約185公分。

English Sentence: She always wears colourful clothes and has a very unique sense of style.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她總是穿著色彩鮮豔的衣服，有著非常獨特的穿衣風格。

English Sentence: He is one of the kindest and most generous people I have ever met.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他是我見過的最善良、最慷慨的人之一。

English Sentence: She tends to be quite shy at first, but she opens up once she gets to know you.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她一開始往往很害羞，但一旦了解你之後就會敞開心扉。
Grammar / Usage Note: contrast using but with once clause

English Sentence: He is very hardworking and rarely takes a day off.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他非常勤奮，很少請假。
Grammar / Usage Note: adverb of frequency with positive trait

English Sentence: She has a wonderful sense of humour and always makes everyone laugh.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她有很好的幽默感，總是讓大家開懷大笑。
Grammar / Usage Note: describing personality with sense of humour

English Sentence: He speaks very quietly, so you have to listen carefully when he talks.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他說話很輕聲，所以聽他說話時要仔細聆聽。

English Sentence: She is incredibly patient with children and would make a wonderful teacher.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她對孩子非常有耐心，會是一位很出色的老師。

English Sentence: He is the kind of person who always puts others before himself.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他是那種總是先考慮別人的人。
Grammar / Usage Note: the kind of person who clause

English Sentence: She is very driven and ambitious; she always knows what she wants.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她非常有上進心和抱負，她總是知道自己想要什麼。

English Sentence: He can be a bit impatient at times, but his heart is always in the right place.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他有時可能有點不耐煩，但他的出發點總是好的。

English Sentence: She is extremely creative and is always coming up with new ideas.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她非常有創意，總是不斷想出新點子。
Grammar / Usage Note: always + present continuous for habitual behaviour

English Sentence: He is a very private person and doesn't like to share too much about himself.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他是一個非常注重隱私的人，不喜歡分享太多關於自己的事。

English Sentence: She is well-organised and always meets her deadlines without any stress.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她很有條理，總是能在沒有壓力的情況下按時完成任務。

English Sentence: He has a very calming presence; just being around him makes you feel at ease.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他有一種非常令人安心的氣質；只要在他身邊就會讓你感到放鬆。

English Sentence: She grew up in a small town, which shaped her down-to-earth personality.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在一個小鎮長大，這塑造了她腳踏實地的個性。

English Sentence: He volunteers every weekend, which shows how caring he really is.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他每個週末都做志工，這顯示了他有多體貼。
Grammar / Usage Note: which clause to draw a conclusion

English Sentence: She is the most reliable person on the team; everyone trusts her completely.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她是團隊中最可靠的人；大家都完全信任她。

English Sentence: He tends to overthink things, but that also means he is very thorough.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他往往想太多，但這也意味著他非常細心。

English Sentence: The city is very modern and has a lot to offer in terms of culture and entertainment.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這座城市非常現代化，在文化和娛樂方面有很多可以提供的東西。
Grammar / Usage Note: in terms of for specifying area

English Sentence: The village is small and quiet, surrounded by rolling hills and green fields.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個村莊小而寧靜，四周是連綿起伏的山丘和綠色的田野。

English Sentence: The beach is stunning, with white sand and crystal-clear water.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這片海灘令人驚嘆，有白色的沙灘和清澈見底的海水。
Grammar / Usage Note: with clause adding detail to a description

English Sentence: The restaurant has a very cosy atmosphere and the lighting is warm and soft.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這家餐廳氣氛非常溫馨，燈光溫暖柔和。

English Sentence: The park is always busy on weekends, especially in summer.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個公園週末總是很熱鬧，尤其是夏天。

English Sentence: The city centre can get quite noisy and crowded during peak hours.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 市中心在高峰時段會相當嘈雜和擁擠。

English Sentence: The neighbourhood is very safe and family-friendly, which is why we chose to live here.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個社區非常安全且適合家庭居住，這也是我們選擇住在這裡的原因。
Grammar / Usage Note: which is why clause for explanation

English Sentence: The museum is huge and you could easily spend an entire day there.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個博物館非常大，你可以輕鬆地在那裡待上一整天。

English Sentence: The countryside is breathtaking in autumn when the leaves change colour.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 秋天樹葉變色的時候，鄉村風景令人嘆為觀止。

English Sentence: The old town is full of narrow cobblestone streets and historic buildings.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 舊城區到處是狹窄的鵝卵石街道和歷史性建築。

English Sentence: It is a very walkable city; you can get almost anywhere on foot within twenty minutes.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這是一個非常適合步行的城市；幾乎任何地方步行都可以在二十分鐘內到達。
Grammar / Usage Note: walkable with semi-colon for supporting detail

English Sentence: The apartment is on the tenth floor and has a beautiful view of the skyline.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這間公寓在十樓，可以欣賞到美麗的天際線景色。
Grammar / Usage Note: has a view of for scenic description

English Sentence: The area has changed a lot over the past ten years; it used to be quite run-down.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個地區在過去十年裡變化了很多；它過去曾經相當破舊。
Grammar / Usage Note: used to + infinitive for past contrast

English Sentence: The market is open every morning from six until noon and sells everything from fresh fruit to handmade crafts.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個市場每天早上六點到中午開放，販售從新鮮水果到手工藝品的各種東西。
Grammar / Usage Note: from... to for range with time detail

English Sentence: The hotel is perfectly located, just a short walk from the beach and the main shopping street.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這家酒店位置絕佳，距海灘和主要購物街僅需步行片刻。
Grammar / Usage Note: just a short walk from for proximity

English Sentence: The town has a very strong sense of community; everyone knows each other.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個小鎮有非常強烈的社區意識；大家都互相認識。
Grammar / Usage Note: sense of community with semi-colon support

English Sentence: The forest is incredibly peaceful; the only sounds you can hear are birds and the wind.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這片森林非常寧靜；你能聽到的只有鳥叫聲和風聲。
Grammar / Usage Note: the only... are for emphasis on quietness

English Sentence: The stadium can hold over fifty thousand people and has a fantastic atmosphere on match days.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個體育場可以容納超過五萬人，比賽日的氣氛非常棒。

English Sentence: The café is tucked away on a side street, which makes it feel like a hidden gem.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這家咖啡廳藏在一條小巷裡，讓人感覺像是發現了一個隱藏的寶藏。
Grammar / Usage Note: tucked away for hidden location description

English Sentence: The library is a beautiful old building that was built over a hundred years ago.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這座圖書館是一棟美麗的老建築，建造於一百多年前。
Grammar / Usage Note: that relative clause for historical context

English Sentence: I went to the supermarket this morning and bought everything on my list.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我今天早上去了超市，買了清單上的所有東西。

English Sentence: She called me last night and told me some exciting news.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她昨晚打電話給我，告訴我一些令人興奮的消息。

English Sentence: We had a great time at the party last weekend.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們上週末的派對玩得很開心。
Grammar / Usage Note: simple past with had a great time

English Sentence: He forgot to bring his wallet and had to borrow money from a friend.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他忘了帶錢包，不得不向朋友借錢。
Grammar / Usage Note: simple past with had to for obligation

English Sentence: I missed the bus this morning and arrived at work twenty minutes late.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我今天早上錯過了公車，上班遲到了二十分鐘。

English Sentence: She finished her project ahead of schedule, which impressed her manager.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她提前完成了她的專案，讓她的經理留下深刻印象。

English Sentence: We visited my grandparents last month and stayed for a whole week.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們上個月去探望了祖父母，並且住了整整一個星期。

English Sentence: He broke his leg while playing football and had to use crutches for six weeks.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他踢足球時摔斷了腿，不得不用拐杖走路六週。

English Sentence: I spent the entire afternoon looking for my keys before I finally found them in my coat pocket.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我花了整個下午找我的鑰匙，最後終於在大衣口袋裡找到了。

English Sentence: She graduated from university last June and started working the following month.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她去年六月從大學畢業，下個月就開始工作了。
Grammar / Usage Note: sequence of life events in past

English Sentence: We got caught in a heavy rainstorm on our way home and got completely soaked.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們回家途中遇上了大暴雨，全身都濕透了。
Grammar / Usage Note: get caught in for unexpected event

English Sentence: He worked at that company for ten years before deciding to start his own business.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在那家公司工作了十年，然後決定自己創業。

English Sentence: I watched a documentary last night that completely changed the way I think about climate change.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我昨晚看了一部紀錄片，完全改變了我對氣候變遷的看法。

English Sentence: She won first place in the competition and received a scholarship as a prize.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在比賽中獲得了第一名，並獲得了獎學金作為獎勵。

English Sentence: We went on a camping trip last summer and it rained almost every day.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們去年夏天去露營，幾乎每天都下雨。

English Sentence: He proposed to her on their anniversary and she said yes immediately.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在他們的週年紀念日向她求婚，她立刻答應了。

English Sentence: I had already eaten dinner by the time she arrived, so I just had some dessert.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她到的時候我已經吃過晚飯了，所以我只吃了點甜點。

English Sentence: She had been studying English for three years before she moved to London.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她搬到倫敦之前已經學了三年的英文。
Grammar / Usage Note: past perfect continuous for duration before event

English Sentence: We were having dinner when the power suddenly went out.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們正在吃晚飯時，電突然斷了。
Grammar / Usage Note: past continuous interrupted by past simple

English Sentence: He had just fallen asleep when his phone started ringing.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他剛剛睡著，手機就開始響了。
Grammar / Usage Note: had just + past simple for immediate interruption

English Sentence: I am currently working on a very challenging project.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我目前正在進行一個非常具有挑戰性的專案。

English Sentence: She is looking for a new apartment because her current lease is ending.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她正在找新公寓，因為她目前的租約快到期了。

English Sentence: He has been living in this city for five years now.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他已經在這座城市住了五年了。

English Sentence: We are thinking about moving to a bigger house.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們在考慮搬到更大的房子。

English Sentence: She has just finished her exams and is feeling very relieved.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她剛剛考完試，感到非常鬆了一口氣。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect just with current feeling

English Sentence: I have been feeling a bit tired lately, probably because of the long hours at work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我最近感覺有點疲倦，可能是因為工作時間很長。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous with probable reason

English Sentence: He is working overtime this week because there is a big deadline coming up.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他這週在加班，因為有一個重要的截止日期快到了。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous this week with reason

English Sentence: We have been together for three years and are planning to get married next year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們在一起三年了，計劃明年結婚。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous with future plan

English Sentence: She is doing really well in her new job and has already received a promotion.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在新工作中表現非常出色，已經獲得了晉升。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous + present perfect with already

English Sentence: I have known him since we were in school together.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我從我們一起上學的時候就認識他了。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect with since for long duration

English Sentence: She is taking an online course to improve her skills.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她正在上線上課程來提升她的技能。

English Sentence: He hasn't been sleeping well recently due to the stress at work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他最近因為工作壓力睡眠不太好。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect negative with recent and due to

English Sentence: We are renovating our kitchen at the moment, so everything is a bit chaotic.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們目前正在翻修廚房，所以一切都有點混亂。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous at the moment with result

English Sentence: She has applied for several jobs and is waiting to hear back.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她已經申請了幾份工作，正在等待回覆。

English Sentence: I am trying to cut down on caffeine, which is harder than I expected.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我正在嘗試減少咖啡因的攝取，這比我預期的要難。

English Sentence: He is learning to play the guitar and is really enjoying it.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他正在學彈吉他，非常享受其中。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous for skill in progress

English Sentence: We are saving up for a holiday abroad next year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們正在為明年的海外假期存錢。

English Sentence: She has been working here for six months and has already made a great impression.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在這裡工作了六個月，已經留下了很好的印象。

English Sentence: I am not feeling well today, so I might leave work early.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我今天感覺不舒服，所以我可能會提早離開工作。
Grammar / Usage Note: present continuous for current state + might

English Sentence: He has recently taken up cycling as a way to stay fit.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他最近開始騎自行車，作為保持健康的方式。
Grammar / Usage Note: recently + present perfect with as a way to

English Sentence: I am going to apply for a promotion next month.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我下個月要去申請晉升。
Grammar / Usage Note: going to for planned future action

English Sentence: She is planning to travel around Europe after she graduates.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她畢業後計劃環遊歐洲。
Grammar / Usage Note: plan to + infinitive with after clause

English Sentence: We will probably move to a bigger city within the next two years.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們可能在未來兩年內搬到更大的城市。

English Sentence: He is thinking about going back to university to get a master's degree.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在考慮回大學攻讀碩士學位。

English Sentence: I hope to have my own business by the time I'm forty.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我希望在我四十歲之前擁有自己的事業。
Grammar / Usage Note: hope to + infinitive with by the time

English Sentence: She is going to start learning Japanese next semester.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她下學期要開始學日文。
Grammar / Usage Note: going to for decided future plan

English Sentence: We are flying to Barcelona next Friday for a long weekend.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們下週五飛往巴塞隆納度長週末。

English Sentence: He is going to retire in five years and travel the world.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他打算在五年後退休並環遊世界。
Grammar / Usage Note: going to + and for linked future plans

English Sentence: I will call you as soon as I hear any news.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我一有任何消息就會打電話給你。

English Sentence: She is due to give a presentation at the conference next week.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她預計下週在會議上做一個簡報。
Grammar / Usage Note: be due to for scheduled events

English Sentence: We are hoping to buy our first home before the end of the year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們希望在年底前買到我們的第一套房子。
Grammar / Usage Note: be hoping to for optimistic plan

English Sentence: He has decided to change careers and is currently retraining.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他已決定轉行，目前正在接受再培訓。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect decision + present continuous action

English Sentence: I am not sure what I will do after I finish this project.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我不確定完成這個專案後我會做什麼。

English Sentence: She is set to become the youngest manager in the company's history.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她將成為公司歷史上最年輕的經理。
Grammar / Usage Note: be set to for imminent achievement

English Sentence: We plan to expand the business into new markets over the next three years.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們計劃在未來三年內將業務拓展到新市場。
Grammar / Usage Note: plan to + infinitive with time frame

English Sentence: He is going to ask her to marry him on their holiday next month.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他打算下個月度假時向她求婚。
Grammar / Usage Note: going to for decided personal plan

English Sentence: I will do my best to finish everything before the deadline.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我會盡力在截止日期前完成所有事情。

English Sentence: She will probably be promoted if she keeps up this level of performance.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果她繼續保持這種表現水準，她可能會獲得晉升。

English Sentence: We are looking forward to seeing everyone at the reunion next year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們期待明年在聚會上見到大家。
Grammar / Usage Note: look forward to + gerund for anticipated event

English Sentence: He is saving up so that he can buy a car by the end of the year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他正在存錢，以便在年底前能買到一輛車。

English Sentence: I always make a to-do list at the start of each day to stay organised.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每天開始時都會列一份待辦事項清單，以保持條理。

English Sentence: She tends to procrastinate when she has a task she finds boring.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在做無聊的任務時往往會拖延。
Grammar / Usage Note: tend to for habit with when clause

English Sentence: He has a habit of checking his phone first thing in the morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他有早上第一件事就查手機的習慣。

English Sentence: We try to eat dinner together at least four times a week.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們盡量一週至少一起吃四次晚飯。
Grammar / Usage Note: try to with at least + frequency

English Sentence: She rarely misses a gym session; she goes five times a week without fail.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她很少錯過健身房的課；她每週去五次，從不間斷。
Grammar / Usage Note: rarely + semi-colon + without fail for emphasis

English Sentence: I have been in the habit of journalling every night for the past two years.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我過去兩年一直有每晚寫日記的習慣。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect for duration of habit

English Sentence: He tends to work late on Thursdays because that is when reports are due.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他週四往往工作到很晚，因為那是報告截止的時間。

English Sentence: We make it a point to have a family game night every Friday.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們特意安排每個週五晚上舉辦家庭遊戲之夜。
Grammar / Usage Note: make it a point to + infinitive

English Sentence: She always stretches for ten minutes after every workout.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每次鍛鍊後總是會伸展十分鐘。

English Sentence: I have gotten into the habit of meditating for fifteen minutes every morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我已經養成了每天早上冥想十五分鐘的習慣。
Grammar / Usage Note: get into the habit of + gerund

English Sentence: He automatically switches to English whenever he meets someone from abroad.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 每當他遇到來自國外的人時，他會自動切換成英文。

English Sentence: She likes to plan her week on Sunday evenings so she feels prepared.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她喜歡在週日晚上計劃她的一週，這樣她就會感到有準備。
Grammar / Usage Note: so clause for purpose of habit

English Sentence: I find it hard to concentrate unless I have complete silence.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 除非完全安靜，否則我很難集中注意力。

English Sentence: We always prepare our outfits the night before to save time in the morning.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們總是在前一天晚上準備好衣服，以節省早上的時間。

English Sentence: He has a routine of reviewing his goals every Sunday to track his progress.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他每週日都會審視自己的目標，以追蹤進度。

English Sentence: She drinks a glass of warm water with lemon every morning before eating.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每天早上吃東西前都會喝一杯加檸檬的溫水。

English Sentence: I set aside time each week to read something unrelated to work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我每週撥出時間閱讀與工作無關的東西。

English Sentence: He keeps a water bottle on his desk to remind himself to stay hydrated.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他把水瓶放在桌上，提醒自己保持水分。
Grammar / Usage Note: to + infinitive for purpose with remind

English Sentence: We take a short walk after lunch every day; it helps us feel more alert.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們每天午飯後都會短暫散步；這幫助我們保持清醒。

English Sentence: She has been going to the same coffee shop every morning for five years.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她每天早上去同一家咖啡廳已經有五年了。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect continuous for long-standing habit

English Sentence: I think it is important to keep learning new things throughout your life.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我認為一生中不斷學習新事物是很重要的。
Grammar / Usage Note: I think + it is important to

English Sentence: In my opinion, communication is the key to any successful relationship.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 依我看，溝通是任何成功關係的關鍵。

English Sentence: I prefer working in the morning when my mind is fresh.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我更喜歡在早上頭腦清醒的時候工作。

English Sentence: She would rather stay home and read than go to a noisy party.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她寧可待在家裡看書，也不願去嘈雜的派對。

English Sentence: I find it much easier to concentrate when I work from home.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現在家工作時更容易集中注意力。

English Sentence: Personally, I think social media does more harm than good.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我個人認為社群媒體弊大於利。
Grammar / Usage Note: Personally + I think with comparative expression

English Sentence: He believes that experience is more valuable than academic qualifications.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他認為經驗比學歷更有價值。

English Sentence: From my point of view, it is better to be honest even if it is uncomfortable.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 從我的角度來看，即使不舒服，誠實也是更好的選择。
Grammar / Usage Note: From my point of view + even if clause

English Sentence: I am not a big fan of crowded places; I much prefer quiet, open spaces.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我不太喜歡擁擠的地方；我更喜歡安靜、開闊的空間。
Grammar / Usage Note: not a big fan of + semi-colon contrast

English Sentence: She feels strongly that children should spend more time outdoors.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她強烈認為孩子們應該多花時間在戶外。

English Sentence: I tend to prefer quality over quantity when it comes to friendships.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 說到友誼，我傾向於重質不重量。
Grammar / Usage Note: tend to prefer with when it comes to

English Sentence: As far as I am concerned, money cannot buy happiness.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 就我而言，金錢買不到幸福。
Grammar / Usage Note: As far as I am concerned + clause

English Sentence: I strongly believe that everyone deserves equal opportunities regardless of their background.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我強烈相信每個人都應該得到平等的機會，無論其背景如何。
Grammar / Usage Note: strongly believe that with regardless of

English Sentence: In my view, the most important quality in a leader is empathy.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 依我看，領導者最重要的素質是同理心。

English Sentence: I would much rather have a few close friends than many acquaintances.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我寧願有幾個親密的朋友，也不願有許多點頭之交。
Grammar / Usage Note: would much rather than for strong preference

English Sentence: She thinks that learning a second language opens up so many opportunities.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她認為學習第二語言會開啟很多機會。
Grammar / Usage Note: thinks that with so many for emphasis

English Sentence: To be honest, I am not entirely convinced by that argument.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 說實話，我對那個論點並不完全信服。
Grammar / Usage Note: To be honest with not entirely for polite disagreement

English Sentence: I find that taking regular breaks actually improves my productivity.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現定期休息實際上會提高我的生產力。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find that with actually for surprising truth

English Sentence: He is of the opinion that technology has made people less connected.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他認為科技讓人們的連結變少了。
Grammar / Usage Note: be of the opinion that for formal opinion

English Sentence: I believe that small acts of kindness can make a huge difference in someone's day.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我相信小小的善意可以讓某人的一天大不相同。
Grammar / Usage Note: believe that with can for possibility
